channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: We called 1-800-ChatGPT to see if OpenAI would ruin Christmas
item.description: OpenAI just launched a way to call ChatGPT over the phone as a means of introducing people to the generative AI service. Users in the U.S. and Canada can call 1-800-CHATGPT (1-800-242-8478) to chat with the service or ask questions for up to 15 minutes for free per month.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: LA Zoo hatches first-ever perentie lizards, one of largest lizard species in the world
item.description: Two new perentie lizards have hatched at the Los Angeles Zoo, the first of their species to be bred there.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple delivery hubs. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union are holding a strike at seven of the company’s delivery hubs less than a week before Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Giant sloths and mastodons coexisted with humans for millennia in Americas, new discoveries suggest
item.description: New discoveries from several archaeological sites in North and South America suggest that ancient people first arrived in the New World much earlier than scientists once thought.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk floods X with spending bill misinformation
item.description: Billionaire tech mogul Elon Musk unleashed a flood of misinformation about Congress’s end-of-year funding deal Wednesday, as he pushed Republicans to torpedo the stopgap bill.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Trump team affirms he's leader of GOP amid 'President Musk' barbs
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team pushed back against Democratic lawmakers who suggested Elon Musk is the "real leader" of the Republican Party after his aggressive pressure campaign against a stopgap spending measure.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The AI journey to discovery and achieving IT mastery
item.description: What if artificial intelligence (AI) could prevent 1,000 potential outages and improve IT service health and delivery by more than 75%? IT teams would sleep better, but that’s just the start.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Got an Intel Core Ultra 200S CPU? These are the patches you need to help gaming performance – with one more update coming in January 2025
item.description: Intel's had a bit of a nightmare with Arrow Lake, but has been commendably transparent in putting things right.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How OpenAI’s Jerry Tworek found a new way forward for large language models
item.description: The impressive intelligence gains in OpenAI’s models over time have mainly come from training them with progressively more training data, for longer amounts of time, and with massive computing power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Blinken announces aid for Sudan and talks about AI risks at UN Security Council
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken has announced $200 million in humanitarian aid for conflict-torn Sudan during his likely final trip to the United Nations while in office.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: IT firms recognise the gender gap, but not all have plans to fix it
item.description: Businesses aren’t doing enough to support women in tech, but here’s what they could be doing to contribute.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: FAQ: Getting started with Bluesky
item.description: What is Bluesky? Bluesky is a “microblogging” social media network similar to X (formerly Twitter), where people can publish posts with a maximum of 300 characters, as well as follow others, create lists, like posts, and so on.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Data on animal movements help Hungarian researchers create a swarm of autonomous drones
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Moving in a dense cloud, like throngs of people walking across a crowded public square, 100 drones maneuver through the night sky in Hungary's capital, the result of over a decade of research and experimentation that scientists believe could change the future of unmanned fli
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bernie Sanders blasts 'President Elon Musk' for derailing spending deal
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) took aim at tech mogul Elon Musk for saying any lawmaker supporting the newly unveiled end-of-the-year continuing resolution funding deal should be voted out of office. "Democrats and Republicans spent months negotiating a bipartisan agreement to fund our government.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Snapchat is overhauling how influencers earn money on the platform
item.description: “I’m sorry WHO is using snapchat in the big year of 2024,” one X user asked. Turns out, a lot of people and for some, it’s proving lucrative. Influencer Julieanna Goddard, better known as YesJulz, recently posted her impressive Snapchat earnings to social media in response to the question.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Flipboard’s new app is a discovery engine for the decentralized web
item.description: Fourteen years ago, when the iPad was new and bursting with unfulfilled promise, an app called Flipboard debuted.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: McConnell urges Supreme Court to reject TikTok's bid to delay ban
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) filed an amicus brief with the Supreme Court on Wednesday, urging it to reject TikTok’s request to delay a law that could ban the app next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: VA EHR seeing fewer crashes amid plans to resume rollout in FY 2025
item.description: VA’s Oracle-Cerner EHR has seen a 50% decrease in crashes and lags since the VA completed its last round of updates in August. The post VA EHR seeing fewer crashes amid plans to resume rollout in FY 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Slack adds Agentforce ‘hub’ for AI agents
item.description: Slack is adding a new way to access AI agents created on parent company Salesforce’s Agentforce platform, with a library of options available in the collaboration app. Agentforce, which launched in October, is a tool to build AI agents that answer questions and automate tasks for users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple’s Craig Federighi is finding the practical side of AI
item.description: Few companies have the potential to get consumers hooked on AI like Apple. More than one billion people worldwide use an iPhone, and they’ll inevitably become acquainted with Apple Intelligence—the catch-all term for Apple’s suite of generative AI features—as they upgrade to a compatible device.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: How AI can help with your last-minute holiday shopping
item.description: If you’re still working on your holiday shopping—or looking for a treat for yourself—you might consider using artificial intelligence tools to help you find the perfect purchase.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: OpenAI responds to Elon Musk's lawsuit
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EPA grants California authority to ban sales of new gas cars by 2035. Action faces reversal by Trump
item.description: The California rule is stricter than a federal rule adopted this year that tightens emissions standards but does not require sales of electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: CompTIA launches expert-level cybersecurity certification
item.description: CompTIA this week made available its CompTIA SecurityX certification program, which is an updated version of the CompTIA Advanced Security Practitioner (CASP+) cert and part of CompTIA’s Xpert Series of certifications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: On Arm PC return rates and CEO posturing
item.description: With her claim that retailers are seeing high returns of Arm PCs, Intel interim CEO Michelle Johnston Holthaus appears to be trying to scare buyers off the rival processor architecture. But enterprise buyers who look before they leap have little to fear.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Youtube has a new tool to detect AI-faked celebrities
item.description: Fake AI clips of celebrities are becoming increasingly common, something that affects, among other things, artists and actors who have their voices and looks cloned without their permission.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Barcelona subway recycles energy from braking into power to charge electric cars
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — The Barcelona subway grinds to a halt, the doors slide open and commuters pour out to go about their daily business. Little do they know that as they do so, a burst of energy is sent up to street level to help charge an electric car.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: IT/OT convergence propels zero-trust security efforts
item.description: Zero-trust security is essential to enterprises that are converging operational technology (OT) with IT infrastructure. New research from Enterprise Management Associates (EMA) identified how this convergence influences zero-trust strategy and implementation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nima Momeni found guilty in Cash App CEO's death
item.description: Nima Momeni testified he was defending himself when Cash App CEO Bob Lee was stabbed and later died at a local hospital
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Groups urge Trump to reinforce National Cyber Director role
item.description: The role of the White House national cyber director has emerged as a key issue for former cyber officials as the Trump administration prepares to take over. The post Groups urge Trump to reinforce National Cyber Director role first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network
